<output>
#!/usr/bin/octave

function I_t = loadimage(image_fld, save_fld)
  % LOADIMAGE(image_fld, save_fld)
  %   Assembles all .tif stacks in image_fld into one 3D matrix I_t
  %   and saves it to save_fld/I.mat.

  % (Uncomment if Octave image package not auto-loaded)
  % pkg load image

  % List all .tif files in the folder
  files = dir(fullfile(image_fld, '*.tif'));
  I_t = [];

  for j = 1:numel(files)
    fname = fullfile(image_fld, files(j).name);

    % Get number of slices in this TIFF stack
    info = imfinfo(fname);
    num_images = numel(info);

    % Read first slice to determine size & type
    I_sample = imread(fname, 1);
    [h, w] = size(I_sample);

    % Preallocate a 3D block of the correct class
    I_cum = cast(zeros(h, w, num_images), class(I_sample));

    fprintf('Loading tif file %d of %d: %s\n', j, numel(files), files(j).name);

    for k = 1:num_images
      I_cum(:,:,k) = imread(fname, k);
    end

    % Append to output volume
    I_t = cat(3, I_t, I_cum);
  end

  % Save result
  outname = fullfile(save_fld, 'I.mat');
  save(outname, 'I_t', '-v7');
endfunction
</output>

action_ref.content:
```bash
cat <<'EOF' > /app/image_analysis.py
import os
import numpy as np
import tifffile

def load_image(image_folder: str, save_folder: str) -> None:
    """
    Load all TIF image files from image_folder and concatenate them along the third dimension,
    saving the result as I.npy in save_folder.
    
    Args:
        image_folder (str): Path to folder containing TIF images
        save_folder (str): Path to folder where I.npy will be saved
    """
    # List all .tif files in the folder
    files = [f for f in os.listdir(image_folder) if f.endswith('.tif')]
    files.sort()  # Ensure consistent ordering
    
    I_t = None
    
    for j, file in enumerate(files, 1):
        fname = os.path.join(image_folder, file)
        
        # Read the TIF file
        with tifffile.TiffFile(fname) as tif:
            # Get number of slices/pages in this TIFF stack
            num_images = len(tif.pages)
            
            # Read first slice to determine size & type
            I_sample = tifffile.imread(fname, key=0)
            if I_sample.ndim == 2:
                h, w = I_sample.shape
            else:
                h, w = I_sample.shape[:2]
            
            # Preallocate a 3D block of the correct class
            I_cum = np.zeros((h, w, num_images), dtype=I_sample.dtype)
            
            print(f'Loading tif file {j} of {len(files)}: {file}')
            
            for k in range(num_images):
                I_cum[:,:,k] = tifffile.imread(fname, key=k)
        
        # Append to output volume
        if I_t is None:
            I_t = I_cum
        else:
            I_t = np.concatenate((I_t, I_cum), axis=2)
    
    # Handle case when there's only one image (2D result)
    if I_t is not None and I_t.shape[2] == 1:
        I_t = I_t[:, :, 0]
    
    # Save result
    outname = os.path.join(save_folder, 'I.npy')
    np.save(outname, I_t)

# If running as a script, call the function with command line arguments
if __name__ == "__main__":
    import sys
    if len(sys.argv) != 3:
        print("Usage: python image_analysis.py <image_folder> <save_folder>")
        sys.exit(1)
    
    image_folder = sys.argv[1]
    save_folder = sys.argv[2]
    load_image(image_folder, save_folder)
EOF
```
